desc: Ostrava is a post-industrial Czech city reinvented through culture venues, music festivals, and accessible day trips into the Beskydy hills.
hls: Strong festivals and nightlife relative to cost
Good rail hub for Poland/Slovakia cross-border trips
Fast access to Beskydy mountain trails

todo.name: Dolní Vítkovice guided visit
todo.desc: Tour converted blast furnaces and industrial architecture.

todo.name: Stodolní Street evening
todo.desc: Sample bars and live-music venues in the core entertainment district.

todo.name: New City Hall tower viewpoint
todo.desc: Use the lookout for skyline and industrial panorama context.

todo.name: Beskydy day hike
todo.desc: Take rail/bus to foothill trailheads for a mountain day.

todo.name: Silesian Ostrava Castle
todo.desc: Visit exhibitions and riverside grounds near the center.

todo.name: Czech-Polish food crawl
todo.desc: Mix Moravian and Silesian dishes in local pubs and bistros.

wifi.note: Excellent urban coverage and strong coworking options.

prac.fltNote: Direct UK flights are limited and route-dependent, so many travelers connect via Prague, Vienna, or Warsaw; summer and Christmas market windows raise demand.
prac.currency: Czechia local currency (check live rates before booking).
prac.lang: Local language plus tourism-level English in main visitor areas.
prac.tz: Check local timezone and DST rules for exact travel dates.
prac.visa: Confirm current entry/transit rules for your passport before booking.
alerts: Winter inversions can worsen air quality in the basin—check AQI if sensitive to pollution.
Festival weekends (especially Colours of Ostrava) sell out central hotels early.
Late-night public transport is good but less frequent outside core tram corridors.

sweet: May-Jun and Sep for mild weather and lower crowding than midsummer event peaks.
